title: Equal Employment Opportunity Violations | OEEO | CDC
text:
iscrimination Act of 2020 ensures federal agencies are held accountable in adhering to anti-discrimination laws. In the event that an employee is denied an equal employment opportunity, they may file an EEO complaint to OEEO within 45 days of the incident.
Notification 
The Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) is deeply committed to building a workplace that advances equal employment opportunity (EEO).
To best realize EEO all CDC employees and applicants, must be treated fairly and consistently on the basis of merit. As such, no employee will be denied equal employment opportunity, subjected to harassment, retaliated against for engaging in protected EEO activity, or denied equal pay or compensation because of race, color, national origin (including ethnicity, accent, and use of a language other than English), religion (including reasonable accommodation of religious beliefs or practices), physical or mental disability (including reasonable accommodation of physical or mental disability), medical condition, genetic information, sex (including pregnancy, childbirth, lactation, and related medical conditions), age, or any other prohibited factor.
A person wishing to file an EEO complaint must contact an EEO counselor of the Office of Equal Employment Opportunity (OEEO) within 45 calendar days of the most recent incident of discrimination (including retaliation). Failu
